company.what_they_do: 3rd Space is a curated dinner and art exhibition series — and growing community — for young, creative leaders, primarily based in New York City. Each dinner (~30 guests) features a chef cooking the meal, a visual artist whose work decorates the space, a live musician, and an actor or speaker sharing their story. The brand also operates a careers page (careers.3rdspace.xyz) listing off-record roles from companies in its community, and a Substack newsletter (3rdspace3rdspace.substack.com) where Jake writes about notable people he meets and jobs worth pursuing. The long-term vision Jake has articulated is to build a next-generation membership club — a 'SoHo House for the next generation.'
raw_notes: Homepage (3rdspace.xyz) shows a Careers section: curated roles from community companies, many off-record, with founder emails as application links. Signed '© 2026 3rd Space.'
Subdomain mail.3rdspace.xyz: 'We connect exceptional young people over dinner in NYC. Periodically sharing jobs, people, and events here.'
Subdomain careers.3rdspace.xyz: 'Roles from companies in the 3rd Space community.'
Substack (3rdspace3rdspace.substack.com): Jake writes about people worth watching, career-changing jobs, and dinner recaps. 'We host dinners and write about the people we meet.'

SponsorMyEvent listing: 3rd Space events described as dinner + art exhibition series; events held at notable NYC venues (Surrogate's Court mentioned). Combined 2M+ impressions per dinner claimed. Guests include Emmy and Oscar winners, emerging chefs, directors, photographers.

Third-party interview (caseyraeborella.com, ~2025): 'at just over a year old, 3rd Space has blossomed into something special.' Each dinner ~30 guests. Dozens of professional and personal connections have come out of events.

Gen-Zine article (January 2025): Conceptual inspiration drawn from 15th-century Florence / Medici family model of investing in artists. Name 'third space' refers to sociological concept of a communal gathering place beyond work and home.

Substack (May 2026 post): Jake is expanding into music management alongside 3rd Space dinners — in a 60-day trial as manager for a past 3rd Space music performer, partnering with Jon Schulder / Triptych.
Charges tickets for dinners; philosophy is that 'skin in the game' improves the ecosystem quality.

individual.name: Jake Sacks
notes: USC alumnus (University of Southern California); started 3rd Space while still a student, initially hosting dinners for startup founders on campus with USC funding.
Founded USC's Founders Society — grew it into what he describes as the largest undergraduate founders society in the country, sponsored by the university and 5+ VC funds.
Was brought on as a partner at the largest student-run VC on the West Coast; quickly became Director of Events.
Past internships at Basic.Space, Ernst & Young, and a16z.
Hosted one of the most-cited LA Tech Week events in 2022.
Recently relocated from LA to New York City to scale 3rd Space in NYC.
Self-described as 'a host, a curator, and an enabler of visions'; obsessive about finding and connecting exceptional people before they become widely known.
Expanding into music management (May 2026 Substack post) as an adjacent venture alongside 3rd Space.
Long-term goal: build the 'next generational membership club' — essentially a reimagined SoHo House for young creatives.
Described by a peer Substack writer as being 'on another level.'
